revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Kuroyu Onsen]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''黒湯温泉'''（くろゆおんせん）は、[[秋田県]][[仙北郡]][[田沢湖町]]（旧国[[羽後国]]）[[乳頭温泉郷]]にある[[温泉]]。 == アクセス == *鉄道：[[秋田新幹線]][[田沢湖駅]]よりバスで約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分。 == 泉質 == *硫黄泉 温泉名は黒湯だが、温泉の色は青みががった乳白色である。 == 温泉街 == 温泉郷の最奥部に、一軒宿の「黒湯温泉」が存在する。茅葺、杉皮葺屋根の鄙びた外観が特徴である。古くから[[湯治#.E6.B9.AF.E6.B2.BB.E5.A0.B4|湯治場]]であった伝統から、観光地化した現在でも旅館部の他に自炊部が存在する。 == 歴史 == 開湯は約300年前。温泉郷内では[[鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)|鶴の湯温泉]]に次ぐ歴史がある。鶴の湯に因んで亀の湯の別称があった。古くは[[秋田藩]]の湯治場であった。
現在の乳頭温泉郷は鶴の湯が最も人気が有るが、以前は黒湯が一番人気を誇っていた。 == 関連項目 == *[[温泉]]、[[温泉街]]、[[日本の温泉地一覧]] [[category:温泉|くろゆおんせん]]

'''黒湯温泉'''（くろゆおんせん）は、[[秋田県]][[仙北郡]][[田沢湖町]]（旧国[[羽後国]]）[[乳頭温泉郷]]にある[[温泉]]。 == アクセス == *鉄道：[[秋田新幹線]][[田沢湖駅]]よりバスで約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分。 == 泉質 == *硫黄泉 温泉名は黒湯だが、温泉の色は青みががった乳白色である。 == 温泉街 == 温泉郷の最奥部に、一軒宿の「黒湯温泉」が存在する。茅葺、杉皮葺屋根の鄙びた外観が特徴である。古くから[[湯治#.E6.B9.AF.E6.B2.BB.E5.A0.B4|湯治場]]であった伝統から、観光地化した現在でも旅館部の他に自炊部が存在する。 == 歴史 == 開湯は約300年前。温泉郷内では[[鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)|鶴の湯温泉]]に次ぐ歴史がある。鶴の湯に因んで亀の湯の別称があった。古くは[[秋田藩]]の湯治場であった。
現在の乳頭温泉郷は鶴の湯が最も人気が有るが、以前は黒湯が一番人気を誇っていた。 == 関連項目 == *[[温泉]]、[[温泉街]]、[[日本の温泉地一覧]]
[[category:温泉|くろゆおんせん]] [[Category:秋田県の建築物・観光名所|くろゆおんせん]]

'''黒湯温泉'''（くろゆおんせん）は、[[秋田県]][[仙北市]]（旧国[[羽後国]]）[[乳頭温泉郷]]にある[[温泉]]。 == アクセス == *鉄道：[[秋田新幹線]][[田沢湖駅]]よりバスで約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分。
== 泉質 == *硫黄泉 温泉名は黒湯だが、温泉の色は青みががった乳白色である。
== 温泉街 == 温泉郷の最奥部に、一軒宿の「黒湯温泉」が存在する。茅葺、杉皮葺屋根の鄙びた外観が特徴である。古くから[[湯治#湯治場|湯治場]]であった伝統から、観光地化した現在でも旅館部の他に自炊部が存在する。
== 歴史 == 開湯は約300年前。温泉郷内では[[鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)|鶴の湯温泉]]に次ぐ歴史がある。鶴の湯に因んで亀の湯の別称があった。古くは[[秋田藩]]の湯治場であった。
現在の乳頭温泉郷は鶴の湯が最も人気が有るが、以前は黒湯が一番人気を誇っていた。
== 関連項目 == *[[温泉]]、[[温泉街]]、[[日本の温泉地一覧]]
[[category:温泉|くろゆおんせん]] [[Category:秋田県の建築物・観光名所|くろゆおんせん]]

{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 黒湯温泉 |画像 = 雪の黒湯温泉Img354.jpg |コメント = 雪の黒湯温泉 |所在地 = [[秋田県]][[仙北市]] |交通アクセス = 鉄道：[[秋田新幹線]][[田沢湖駅]]よりバスで約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分 |泉質 = [[硫黄泉]] |泉温 = |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = [http://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉公式ホームページ] |特記事項 = }}
'''黒湯温泉'''（くろゆおんせん）は、[[秋田県]][[仙北市]]（旧国[[出羽国]]、[[明治]]以降は[[羽後国]]）[[乳頭温泉郷]]にある[[温泉]]。 == アクセス == * 鉄道：[[秋田新幹線]][[田沢湖駅]]よりバスで約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分。
== 泉質 == * [[硫黄泉]] 温泉名は黒湯だが、温泉の色は青みがかった乳白色である。
== 温泉街 == 温泉郷の最奥部に、一軒宿の「黒湯温泉」が存在する。茅葺、杉皮葺屋根の鄙びた外観が特徴である。古くから[[湯治#湯治場|湯治場]]であった伝統から、観光地化した現在でも旅館部の他に自炊部が存在する。
== 歴史 == 開湯は約300年前。温泉郷内では[[鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)|鶴の湯温泉]]に次ぐ歴史がある。鶴の湯に因んで亀の湯の別称があった。古くは[[秋田藩]]の湯治場であった。
現在の乳頭温泉郷は鶴の湯が最も人気が有るが、以前は黒湯が一番人気を誇っていた。
[[1967年|昭和42年]][[10月19日]]、乳頭温泉郷の一部として[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。
== 外部リンク == * [http://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉公式ホームページ]
== 関連項目 == {{ウィキポータルリンク|温泉}} *[[温泉]]、[[温泉街]]、[[外湯]]、[[温泉番付]] *[[日本の温泉地一覧]] - [[日本の温泉画像一覧]]

== 泉質 == * [[硫黄泉]] 温泉名は黒湯だが、温泉の色は青みがかった乳白色である。
== 歴史 == [[File:Kuroyu Onsen 03.jpg|thumb|270px|<center>湯は乳白色の[[硫黄泉]]</center>]] 開湯は約300年前。温泉郷内では[[鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)|鶴の湯温泉]]に次ぐ歴史がある。鶴の湯に因んで亀の湯の別称があった。古くは[[秋田藩]]の湯治場であった。
現在の乳頭温泉郷は鶴の湯が最も人気が有るが、以前は黒湯が一番人気を誇っていた。

== 温泉街 == 温泉郷の最奥部に、一軒宿の「黒湯温泉」が存在する。茅葺、杉皮葺屋根の鄙びた外観が特徴である。古くから[[湯治#湯治場|湯治場]]であった伝統から、観光地化した現在でも旅館部の他に自炊部が存在する。

revision.text:
{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 黒湯温泉 |画像 = 雪の黒湯温泉Img354.jpg |コメント = 雪の黒湯温泉 |所在地 = [[秋田県]][[仙北市]] |交通アクセス = 鉄道：[[秋田新幹線]][[田沢湖駅]]よりバスで約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分 |泉質 = [[硫黄泉]] |泉温 = |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = [http://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉公式ホームページ] |特記事項 = }} '''黒湯温泉'''（くろゆおんせん）は、[[秋田県]][[仙北市]][[乳頭温泉郷]]にある[[温泉]]。
== 泉質 == * [[硫黄泉]] 温泉名は黒湯だが、温泉の色は青みがかった乳白色である。
== 歴史 == [[File:Kuroyu Onsen 03.jpg|thumb|270px|<center>湯は乳白色の[[硫黄泉]]</center>]] 開湯は約300年前。発見は1674年（延宝2年）頃と推測される。温泉郷内では[[鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)|鶴の湯温泉]]に次ぐ歴史がある。鶴の湯に因んで亀の湯の別称があった。古くは[[秋田藩]]の湯治場であった。
現在の乳頭温泉郷は[[鶴の湯]]が最も人気が有るが、以前は黒湯が一番人気を誇っていた。
[[1967年|昭和42年]][[10月19日]]、乳頭温泉郷の一部として[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定された。
== 温泉地 == 温泉郷の最奥部先達川上流に、一軒宿の「黒湯温泉」が営業する。[[茅葺]]、杉皮葺屋根、周囲の[[雑木林]]から伐りだした木材をそのまま利用した[[露天風呂]]、[[湯小屋]]など、鄙びた外観が特徴である。古くから[[湯治#湯治場|湯治場]]であった伝統を色濃く残し、[[秘湯ブーム]]以降、観光地化した現在でも旅館部の他に自炊部を置く。[[内湯]]を3つ、露天風呂を2つ、[[打たせ湯]]を2つ有する。湯治客のほか、古くから登山客の利用も多い。
== その他 == [[漫画家]]の[[つげ義春]]が、[[昭和40年代]]（1969年5月、1976年9月）に訪れ、当時の[[湯治場]]の雰囲気を4枚の[[イラスト]]にして残している（「旅の景色」、「桃源行」）[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html]。
== ギャラリー == <gallery> File:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|渡り廊下で結ばれた木造板張りの素朴な湯小屋 </gallery>
== アクセス == {{commonscatS|Kuroyu Onsen}} * 鉄道：[[秋田新幹線]][[田沢湖駅]]よりバスで約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分。
== 外部リンク == * [http://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉公式ホームページ]
{{温泉}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くろゆおんせん}}
[[Category:秋田県の温泉]] [[Category:国民保養温泉地]] [[Category:仙北市]]

== その他 == [[漫画家]]の[[つげ義春]]が、[[昭和40年代]]（1969年5月、1976年9月）に訪れ、当時の[[湯治場]]の雰囲気を4枚の[[イラスト]]にして残している（「旅の景色」、「桃源行」）[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html]。
== ギャラリー == <gallery> File:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|渡り廊下で結ばれた木造板張りの素朴な湯小屋 </gallery>
== アクセス == {{Commonscat|Kuroyu Onsen}} * 鉄道：[[秋田新幹線]][[田沢湖駅]]よりバスで約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分。

{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 黒湯温泉 |画像 = 雪の黒湯温泉Img354.jpg |コメント = 杉皮葺屋根の外風呂 |所在地 = [[秋田県]][[仙北市]] |交通アクセス = 鉄道：[[秋田新幹線]][[田沢湖駅]]よりバスで約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分 |泉質 = [[硫黄泉]] |泉温 = |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = [http://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉公式ホームページ] |特記事項 = }} [[File:Kuroyu Onsen 03.jpg|thumb|240px|

]] [[File:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|thumb|240px|
木造板張りの湯小屋
]] '''黒湯温泉'''（くろゆおんせん）は、[[秋田県]][[仙北市]][[乳頭温泉郷]]にある[[温泉]]。温泉郷の最奥部先達川上流に位置する。 == 泉質 == * [[硫黄泉]] 温泉名は黒湯だが、温泉の色は青みがかった乳白色である。 == 歴史 == 開湯は約300年前。発見は1674年（延宝2年）頃と推測される[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html 黒湯温泉] （公式サイト）。温泉郷内では[[鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)|鶴の湯温泉]]に次ぐ歴史があり、鶴の湯に因んで亀の湯の別称があった。古くは[[秋田藩]]の湯治場であった。 現在の乳頭温泉郷は[[鶴の湯]]が最も人気があるが、以前は黒湯が一番人気を誇っていた。[[1967年|昭和42年]][[10月19日]]、乳頭温泉郷の一部として[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定された。 == 温泉地 == 一軒宿の「黒湯温泉」が営業する。 [[茅葺]]、杉皮葺屋根、周囲の[[雑木林]]から伐りだした木材をそのまま利用した[[露天風呂]]、[[湯小屋]]など、鄙びた外観が特徴である。[[内湯]]を3つ、露天風呂を2つ、[[打たせ湯]]を2つ有する。 古くから[[湯治#湯治場|湯治場]]であった伝統を色濃く残し、[[秘湯ブーム]]以降、観光地化した現在でも旅館部の他に自炊部を置く。湯治客のほか、古くから登山客の利用も多い。 なお[[漫画家]]の[[つげ義春]]は、[[昭和40年代]]（1969年5月、1976年9月）に訪れ、当時の[[湯治場]]の雰囲気を4枚の[[イラスト]]にして残している（「旅の景色」、「桃源行」）“[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html つげ義春を旅する]”。 == アクセス == * [[秋田新幹線]][[田沢湖駅]]よりバスで約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分。 == 脚注 == {{Commonscat|Kuroyu Onsen}}

]] '''黒湯温泉'''（くろゆおんせん）は、[[秋田県]][[仙北市]][[乳頭温泉郷]]にある[[温泉]]。温泉郷の最奥部先達川上流に位置する。 == 泉質 == * [[硫黄泉]] 温泉名は黒湯だが、温泉の色は青みがかった乳白色である。 == 歴史 == 開湯は約300年前。発見は[[1674年]]（[[延宝]]2年）頃と推測される[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html 黒湯温泉] （公式サイト）。温泉郷内では[[鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)|鶴の湯温泉]]に次ぐ歴史があり、鶴の湯に因んで亀の湯の別称があった。古くは[[秋田藩]]の湯治場であった。 現在の乳頭温泉郷は[[鶴の湯]]が最も人気があるが、以前は黒湯が一番人気を誇っていた。[[1967年|昭和42年]][[10月19日]]、乳頭温泉郷の一部として[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定された。 == 温泉地 == 一軒宿の「黒湯温泉」が営業する。 [[茅葺]]、杉皮葺屋根、周囲の[[雑木林]]から伐りだした木材をそのまま利用した[[露天風呂]]、[[湯小屋]]など、鄙びた外観が特徴である。[[内湯]]を3つ、露天風呂を2つ、[[打たせ湯]]を2つ有する。 古くから[[湯治#湯治場|湯治場]]であった伝統を色濃く残し、[[秘湯]]ブーム以降、観光地化した現在でも旅館部の他に自炊部を置く。湯治客のほか、古くから登山客の利用も多い。
同じく一軒宿の「[[孫六温泉]]」とは、川を挟んですぐである（但し徒歩のみ）。 現在、乳頭温泉郷の旅館の中で唯一、冬季は休業している（他の６館は通年営業）。 なお[[漫画家]]の[[つげ義春]]は、[[昭和40年代]]（1969年5月、1976年9月）に訪れ、当時の湯治場の雰囲気を4枚の[[イラスト]]にして残している（「旅の景色」、「桃源行」）“[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html つげ義春を旅する]”。 == アクセス == * [[秋田新幹線]][[田沢湖駅]]よりバスで約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分。 * 車の場合、休暇村乳頭温泉郷の前から右手に分け入り、1.3kmほど進むと駐車場がある。駐車場の下に宿がある。 == 脚注 == {{Commonscat|Kuroyu Onsen}}
{{reflist}}

]] [[File:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|thumb|

]] '''黒湯温泉'''（くろゆおんせん）は、[[秋田県]][[仙北市]]の[[乳頭温泉郷]]にある[[温泉]]。温泉郷の最奥部先達川上流に位置する。 == 泉質 == * [[硫黄泉]] 温泉名は黒湯だが、温泉の色は青みがかった乳白色である。 == 歴史 == 開湯は約300年前。発見は[[1674年]]（[[延宝]]2年）頃と推測される[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html 黒湯温泉] （公式サイト）。温泉郷内では[[鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)|鶴の湯温泉]]に次ぐ歴史があり、鶴の湯に因んで亀の湯の別称があった。古くは[[秋田藩]]の湯治場であった。 現在の乳頭温泉郷は[[鶴の湯]]が最も人気があるが、以前は黒湯が一番人気を誇っていた。[[1967年]]（昭和42年）[[10月19日]]、乳頭温泉郷の一部として[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定された。 == 温泉地 == 一軒宿の「黒湯温泉」が営業する。 [[茅葺]]、杉皮葺屋根、周囲の[[雑木林]]から伐りだした木材をそのまま利用した[[露天風呂]]、湯小屋など、鄙びた外観が特徴である。[[内湯]]を3つ、露天風呂を2つ、[[打たせ湯]]を2つ有する。 古くから[[湯治#湯治場|湯治場]]であった伝統を色濃く残し、[[秘湯]]ブーム以降、観光地化した現在でも旅館部の他に自炊部を置く。湯治客のほか、古くから登山客の利用も多い。同じく一軒宿の[[孫六温泉]]とは、川にかかった人道橋を徒歩で渡ってすぐのところにある。 乳頭温泉郷の旅館の中で唯一、冬季は休業している（ほかの6館は通年営業）。 なお[[漫画家]]の[[つげ義春]]は、[[昭和40年代]]（1969年5月、1976年9月）に訪れ、当時の湯治場の雰囲気を4枚の[[イラスト]]にして残している（「旅の景色」、「桃源行」）“[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html つげ義春を旅する]”。 == アクセス == * 鉄道 ** [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[秋田新幹線]]・[[田沢湖線]] [[田沢湖駅]]よりバスで約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分。 * 車 ** [[国道341号]]から[[秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線]]・[[秋田県道194号西山生保内線]]を経由し、休暇村乳頭温泉郷の前から右手に分け入り、1.3kmほど進むと駐車場がある。駐車場の下に宿がある。 == 脚注 == {{Commonscat|Kuroyu Onsen}}
{{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉公式ホームページ] {{温泉}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くろゆおんせん}} [[Category:秋田県の温泉]] [[Category:国民保養温泉地]] [[Category:仙北市の地理]]

]] '''黒湯温泉'''（くろゆおんせん）は、[[秋田県]][[仙北市]]の[[乳頭温泉郷]]にある[[温泉]]。温泉郷の最奥部先達川上流に位置する。乳頭温泉郷で一番高い標高800m地点にある。 == 泉質 == * [[硫黄泉]] 温泉名は[[黒湯]]だが、温泉の色は青みがかった乳白色である。 == 歴史 == 開湯は約300年前。発見は[[1674年]]（[[延宝]]2年）頃と推測される[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html 黒湯温泉] （公式サイト）。温泉郷内では[[鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)|鶴の湯温泉]]に次ぐ歴史があり、鶴の湯に因んで亀の湯の別称があった。古くは[[秋田藩]]の湯治場であった。 獣が病気を癒しに来ることから、諸獣の來留湯(くるゆ)と名付けたという言い伝えや、湯が黒みがかって見えるから黒湯と言ったかどの言い伝えがある。戊辰戦争では国境警備の最前線として、田沢村の農兵や横手の兵隊が藁葺き小屋に泊まって張り番をした。戦前は蛇捕りを副業としている目つきの鋭い老人が湯番をしていて、干した蛇を軒に沢山吊していた千葉治平『ふるさと博物誌』p.57。 現在の乳頭温泉郷は[[鶴の湯]]が最も人気があるが、以前は黒湯が一番人気を誇っていた。[[1967年]]（昭和42年）[[10月19日]]、乳頭温泉郷の一部として[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定された。 == 温泉地 == 一軒宿の「黒湯温泉」が営業する。 [[茅葺]]、杉皮葺屋根、周囲の[[雑木林]]から伐りだした木材をそのまま利用した[[露天風呂]]、湯小屋など、鄙びた外観が特徴である。[[内湯]]を3つ、露天風呂を2つ、[[打たせ湯]]を2つ有する。 古くから[[湯治#湯治場|湯治場]]であった伝統を色濃く残し、[[秘湯]]ブーム以降、観光地化した現在でも旅館部の他に自炊部を置く。湯治客のほか、古くから登山客の利用も多い。同じく一軒宿の[[孫六温泉]]とは、川にかかった人道橋を徒歩で渡ってすぐのところにある。 乳頭温泉郷の旅館の中で唯一、冬季は休業している（ほかの6館は通年営業）。 なお[[漫画家]]の[[つげ義春]]は、[[昭和40年代]]（1969年5月、1976年9月）に訪れ、当時の湯治場の雰囲気を4枚の[[イラスト]]にして残している（「旅の景色」、「桃源行」）“[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html つげ義春を旅する]”。 == アクセス == * 鉄道 ** [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[秋田新幹線]]・[[田沢湖線]] [[田沢湖駅]]よりバスで約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分。 * 車 ** [[国道341号]]から[[秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線]]・[[秋田県道194号西山生保内線]]を経由し、休暇村乳頭温泉郷の前から右手に分け入り、1.3kmほど進むと駐車場がある。駐車場の下に宿がある。 == 脚注 == {{Commonscat|Kuroyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉公式ホームページ] {{温泉}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くろゆおんせん}} [[Category:秋田県の温泉]] [[Category:国民保養温泉地]] [[Category:仙北市の地理]]

]] [[File:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|thumb|
木造板張りの湯小屋
]] '''黒湯温泉'''（くろゆおんせん）は、[[秋田県]][[仙北市]]の[[乳頭温泉郷]]にある[[温泉]]。温泉郷の最奥部先達川上流に位置する。乳頭温泉郷で一番高い標高800m地点にある。 == 泉質 == * [[硫黄泉]] 温泉名は[[黒湯]]だが、温泉の色は青みがかった乳白色である。 == 歴史 == 開湯は約300年前。発見は[[1674年]]（[[延宝]]2年）頃と推測される[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html 黒湯温泉] （公式サイト）。温泉郷内では[[鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)|鶴の湯温泉]]に次ぐ歴史があり、鶴の湯に因んで亀の湯の別称があった。古くは[[秋田藩]]の湯治場であった。 獣が病気を癒しに来ることから、諸獣の來留湯(くるゆ)と名付けたという言い伝えや、湯が黒みがかって見えるから黒湯と言ったなどの言い伝えがある。戊辰戦争では国境警備の最前線として、田沢村の農兵や横手の兵隊が藁葺き小屋に泊まって張り番をした。戦前は蛇捕りを副業としている目つきの鋭い老人が湯番をしていて、干した蛇を軒に沢山吊していた千葉治平『ふるさと博物誌』p.57。 現在の乳頭温泉郷は[[鶴の湯]]が最も人気があるが、以前は黒湯が一番人気を誇っていた。[[1967年]]（昭和42年）[[10月19日]]、乳頭温泉郷の一部として[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定された。 == 温泉地 == 一軒宿の「黒湯温泉」が営業する。 [[茅葺]]、杉皮葺屋根、周囲の[[雑木林]]から伐りだした木材をそのまま利用した[[露天風呂]]、湯小屋など、鄙びた外観が特徴である。[[内湯]]を3つ、露天風呂を2つ、[[打たせ湯]]を2つ有する。 古くから[[湯治#湯治場|湯治場]]であった伝統を色濃く残し、[[秘湯]]ブーム以降、観光地化した現在でも旅館部の他に自炊部を置く。湯治客のほか、古くから登山客の利用も多い。同じく一軒宿の[[孫六温泉]]とは、川にかかった人道橋を徒歩で渡ってすぐのところにある。 乳頭温泉郷の旅館の中で唯一、冬季は休業している（ほかの6館は通年営業）。 なお[[漫画家]]の[[つげ義春]]は、[[昭和40年代]]（1969年5月、1976年9月）に訪れ、当時の湯治場の雰囲気を4枚の[[イラスト]]にして残している（「旅の景色」、「桃源行」）“[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html つげ義春を旅する]”。 == アクセス == * 鉄道 ** [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[秋田新幹線]]・[[田沢湖線]] [[田沢湖駅]]よりバスで約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分。 * 車 ** [[国道341号]]から[[秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線]]・[[秋田県道194号西山生保内線]]を経由し、休暇村乳頭温泉郷の前から右手に分け入り、1.3kmほど進むと駐車場がある。駐車場の下に宿がある。 == 脚注 == {{Commonscat|Kuroyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉公式ホームページ] {{温泉}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くろゆおんせん}} [[Category:秋田県の温泉]] [[Category:国民保養温泉地]] [[Category:仙北市の地理]]

{{otheruses|[[秋田県]][[仙北市]]の黒湯温泉|東京都大田区にある黒い色の温泉|大田区の黒湯温泉}} {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 黒湯温泉 |画像 = 雪の黒湯温泉Img354.jpg |コメント = 杉皮葺屋根の外風呂{{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |所在地 = [[秋田県]][[仙北市]] |座標 = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|9|N|140|48|21|E|type:landmark_region:JP-05|display=inline,title}} |交通アクセス = [[秋田新幹線]][[田沢湖駅]]よりバスで約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分 |泉質 = [[硫黄泉]] |泉温 = |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = [https://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉] |特記事項 = }} '''黒湯温泉'''（くろゆおんせん）は、[[秋田県]][[仙北市]]の[[乳頭温泉郷]]にある[[温泉]]。温泉郷の最奥部先達川上流に位置する。乳頭温泉郷で一番高い標高800m地点にある。
== 泉質 == * [[硫黄泉]] 温泉名は[[黒湯]]だが、温泉の色は青みがかった乳白色である。 [[ファイル:Kuroyu Onsen 03.jpg|thumb|left|湯は乳白色の[[硫黄泉]]]] {{Clearleft}} == 歴史 == 開湯は約300年前。発見は[[1674年]]（[[延宝]]2年）頃と推測される<ref>[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html 黒湯温泉] （公式サイト）</ref>。温泉郷内では[[鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)|鶴の湯温泉]]に次ぐ歴史があり、鶴の湯に因んで亀の湯の別称があった。古くは[[秋田藩]]の湯治場であった。
獣が病気を癒しに来ることから、諸獣の來留湯(くるゆ)と名付けたという言い伝えや、湯が黒みがかって見えるから黒湯と言ったなどの言い伝えがある。戊辰戦争では国境警備の最前線として、田沢村の農兵や横手の兵隊が藁葺き小屋に泊まって張り番をした。戦前は蛇捕りを副業としている目つきの鋭い老人が湯番をしていて、干した蛇を軒に沢山吊していた<ref>千葉治平『ふるさと博物誌』p.57</ref>。
現在の乳頭温泉郷は[[鶴の湯]]が最も人気があるが、以前は黒湯が一番人気を誇っていた。[[1967年]]（昭和42年）[[10月19日]]、乳頭温泉郷の一部として[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定された。
== 温泉地 == [[ファイル:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|thumb|木造板張りの湯小屋]] 一軒宿の「黒湯温泉」が営業する。
[[茅葺]]、杉皮葺屋根、周囲の[[雑木林]]から伐りだした木材をそのまま利用した[[露天風呂]]、湯小屋など、鄙びた外観が特徴である。[[内湯]]を3つ、露天風呂を2つ、[[打たせ湯]]を2つ有する。
古くから[[湯治#湯治場|湯治場]]であった伝統を色濃く残し、[[秘湯]]ブーム以降、観光地化した現在でも旅館部の他に自炊部を置く。湯治客のほか、古くから登山客の利用も多い。同じく一軒宿の[[孫六温泉]]とは、川にかかった人道橋を徒歩で渡ってすぐのところにある。
乳頭温泉郷の旅館の中で唯一、冬季は休業している（ほかの6館は通年営業）。
なお[[漫画家]]の[[つげ義春]]は、[[昭和40年代]]（1969年5月、1976年9月）に訪れ、当時の湯治場の雰囲気を4枚の[[イラスト]]にして残している（「旅の景色」、「桃源行」）<ref>“[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html つげ義春を旅する]”</ref>。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 ** [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[秋田新幹線]]・[[田沢湖線]] [[田沢湖駅]]より[[羽後交通]]の路線バス「乳頭線」で約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分。 * 車 ** [[国道341号]]から[[秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線]]・[[秋田県道194号西山生保内線]]を経由し、休暇村乳頭温泉郷の前から右手に分け入り、1.3kmほど進むと駐車場がある。駐車場の下に宿がある。
== 周辺 == * [[乳頭温泉郷]] ** [[孫六温泉]] ** [[一本松温泉]] ** [[蟹場温泉]] ** [[大釜温泉]] ** [[妙乃湯温泉]] ** [[鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)|鶴の湯温泉]]

なお[[漫画家]]の[[つげ義春]]は、[[昭和40年代]]（1969年5月、1976年9月）に訪れ、当時の湯治場の雰囲気を4枚の[[イラスト]]にして残している（「旅の景色」、「桃源行」）<ref>“[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html つげ義春を旅する]”</ref>。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 ** [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[秋田新幹線]]・[[田沢湖線]] [[田沢湖駅]]より[[羽後交通]]の路線バス「乳頭線」で約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分。 * 車 ** [[国道341号]]から[[秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線]]・[[秋田県道194号西山生保内線]]を経由し、休暇村乳頭温泉郷の前から右手に分け入り、1.3kmほど進むと駐車場がある。駐車場の下に宿がある。
== 周辺 == * [[乳頭温泉郷]] ** [[孫六温泉]] ** [[一本松温泉]] ** [[蟹場温泉]] ** [[大釜温泉]] ** [[妙乃湯温泉]] ** [[鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)|鶴の湯温泉]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kuroyu Onsen}} * [https://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉公式ホームページ] * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/kuroyu.html 黒湯温泉] - 乳頭温泉組合
{{温泉}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くろゆおんせん}} [[Category:秋田県の温泉]] [[Category:国民保養温泉地]] [[Category:仙北市の地理]]

revision.text:
{{otheruses|[[秋田県]][[仙北市]]の黒湯温泉|東京都大田区にある黒い色の温泉|大田区の黒湯温泉}} {{otheruses|秋田県仙北市の黒湯温泉（亀ノ湯）|[[北海道]][[函館市]]にある鉱泉「亀ノ湯」|[[函館山#温泉]]}} {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 黒湯温泉 |画像 = 雪の黒湯温泉Img354.jpg |コメント = 杉皮葺屋根の外風呂{{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |所在地 = [[秋田県]][[仙北市]] |座標 = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|9|N|140|48|21|E|type:landmark_region:JP-05|display=inline,title}} |交通アクセス = [[秋田新幹線]][[田沢湖駅]]よりバスで約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分 |泉質 = [[硫黄泉]] |泉温 = |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = [https://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉] |特記事項 = }} '''黒湯温泉'''（くろゆおんせん）は、[[秋田県]][[仙北市]]の[[乳頭温泉郷]]にある[[温泉]]。温泉郷の最奥部先達川上流に位置する。乳頭温泉郷で一番高い標高800m地点にある。明治中期までは亀ノ湯ともよばれた<ref>谷沢明 [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%BB%92%E6%B9%AF-1162374 "黒湯[温泉<nowiki>]</nowiki>"] 改訂新版 世界大百科事典 平凡社 2024年11月21日閲覧</ref>。
== 泉質 == * [[硫黄泉]] 温泉名は[[黒湯]]だが、温泉の色は青みがかった乳白色である。 [[ファイル:Kuroyu Onsen 03.jpg|thumb|left|湯は乳白色の[[硫黄泉]]]] {{Clearleft}} == 歴史 == 開湯は約300年前。発見は[[1674年]]（[[延宝]]2年）頃と推測される<ref>[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html 黒湯温泉] （公式サイト）</ref>。温泉郷内では[[鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)|鶴の湯温泉]]に次ぐ歴史があり、鶴の湯に因んで亀の湯の別称があった。古くは[[秋田藩]]の湯治場であった。
獣が病気を癒しに来ることから、諸獣の來留湯(くるゆ)と名付けたという言い伝えや、湯が黒みがかって見えるから黒湯と言ったなどの言い伝えがある。戊辰戦争では国境警備の最前線として、田沢村の農兵や横手の兵隊が藁葺き小屋に泊まって張り番をした。戦前は蛇捕りを副業としている目つきの鋭い老人が湯番をしていて、干した蛇を軒に沢山吊していた<ref>千葉治平『ふるさと博物誌』p.57</ref>。
現在の乳頭温泉郷は[[鶴の湯]]が最も人気があるが、以前は黒湯が一番人気を誇っていた。[[1967年]]（昭和42年）[[10月19日]]、乳頭温泉郷の一部として[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定された。
== 温泉地 == [[ファイル:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|thumb|木造板張りの湯小屋]] 一軒宿の「黒湯温泉」が営業する。
[[茅葺]]、杉皮葺屋根、周囲の[[雑木林]]から伐りだした木材をそのまま利用した[[露天風呂]]、湯小屋など、鄙びた外観が特徴である。[[内湯]]を3つ、露天風呂を2つ、[[打たせ湯]]を2つ有する。
古くから[[湯治#湯治場|湯治場]]であった伝統を色濃く残し、[[秘湯]]ブーム以降、観光地化した現在でも旅館部の他に自炊部を置く。湯治客のほか、古くから登山客の利用も多い。同じく一軒宿の[[孫六温泉]]とは、川にかかった人道橋を徒歩で渡ってすぐのところにある。
乳頭温泉郷の旅館の中で唯一、冬季は休業している（ほかの6館は通年営業）。
なお[[漫画家]]の[[つげ義春]]は、[[昭和40年代]]（1969年5月、1976年9月）に訪れ、当時の湯治場の雰囲気を4枚の[[イラスト]]にして残している（「旅の景色」、「桃源行」）<ref>“[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html つげ義春を旅する]”</ref>。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 ** [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[秋田新幹線]]・[[田沢湖線]] [[田沢湖駅]]より[[羽後交通]]の路線バス「乳頭線」で約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分。 * 車 ** [[国道341号]]から[[秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線]]・[[秋田県道194号西山生保内線]]を経由し、休暇村乳頭温泉郷の前から右手に分け入り、1.3kmほど進むと駐車場がある。駐車場の下に宿がある。
== 周辺 == * [[乳頭温泉郷]] ** [[孫六温泉]] ** [[一本松温泉]] ** [[蟹場温泉]] ** [[大釜温泉]] ** [[妙乃湯温泉]] ** [[鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)|鶴の湯温泉]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kuroyu Onsen}} * [https://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉公式ホームページ] * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/kuroyu.html 黒湯温泉] - 乳頭温泉組合

revision.text:
{{otheruses|{{ill|Kuroyu Onsen in Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}|The black-colored hot spring in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|ja|大田区}}|Ōta’s Kuroyu Onsen}} {{otheruses|Kuroyu Onsen (Kame no Yu) in Semboku, Akita|The mineral spring "Kame no Yu" in {{ill|Hakodate, Hokkaido|ja|函館市}}|{{ill|Hakodate Mountain|ja|函館山}}#温泉}} {{Infobox hot spring | name = Kuroyu Onsen | image = 雪の黒湯温泉Img354.jpg | caption = Outdoor bath with a cedar bark roof {{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} | location = {{ill|Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|48|9|N|140|48|21|E|type:landmark_region:JP-05|display=inline,title}} | access = About 55 minutes by bus from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} (Akita Shinkansen), followed by a 20-minute walk. | hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉}} | external link = [https://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉] }}
'''Kuroyu Onsen''' (黒湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Nyuto Onsenkyō|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} in {{ill|Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}. Situated upstream of the Sendatsu River, it lies at the highest elevation within Nyuto Onsenkyō, 800 meters above sea level. Until the mid-Meiji era, it was also known as Kame no Yu.<ref>谷沢明. "[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%BB%92%E6%B9%AF-1162374 黒湯[温泉]]". Revised edition, *World Encyclopedia*, Heibonsha. Retrieved November 21, 2024.</ref>
== Hot Spring Properties == * {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉}} Despite its name "Kuroyu" (literally "Black Hot Spring"), the water is a bluish milky white. [[File:Kuroyu Onsen 03.jpg|thumb|left|Milky white sulfur spring water]] {{Clearleft}}
== History == The hot spring opened approximately 300 years ago, and its discovery is estimated to date back to 1674 (Enpō 2).<ref>[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html Kuroyu Onsen Official Site]</ref> It has the second-longest history in the Nyuto Onsenkyō area after {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}} and was once nicknamed "Kame no Yu" in reference to Tsurunoyu. Historically, it served as a healing retreat for the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}}.
There are legends that the name "Kuroyu" (Black Hot Spring) originated either from the dark appearance of the water or from the belief that animals would come to the spring to heal their ailments. During the Boshin War, it was used as a military outpost, with soldiers from nearby villages stationed in simple straw huts for border defense. Before the war, an elderly caretaker, known for his sharp eyes, used to hang dried snakes under the eaves.<ref>Chiba Chihei. *Furusato Hakubutsushi*, p.57.</ref>
Although Tsurunoyu is now the most popular hot spring in the Nyuto Onsenkyō, Kuroyu enjoyed the highest popularity in earlier times. On October 19, 1967, it was designated a {{ill|National Hot Spring Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of Nyuto Onsenkyō.
== Onsen Facilities == [[File:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|thumb|Wooden bathhouse with plank walls]] Kuroyu Onsen operates as a single lodging facility.
Its rustic appearance, featuring {{ill|thatch|ja|茅葺}} and cedar bark roofs, outdoor baths, and bathhouses built using locally sourced wood, is a hallmark of its charm. The facilities include three indoor baths, two outdoor baths, and two waterfalls for therapeutic bathing.
Retaining its traditional role as a {{ill|Toji|ja|湯治}} (healing retreat), Kuroyu offers both a self-catering section and a standard inn, catering to long-term visitors and climbers. Neighboring {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} is a short walk away, connected by a footbridge over the river.
Kuroyu is the only establishment in Nyuto Onsenkyō that closes during the winter, as the other six inns operate year-round.
The onsen inspired {{ill|Yoshiharu Tsuge|ja|つげ義春}}, a famous manga artist, who visited in May 1969 and September 1976. He depicted its atmosphere in four illustrations titled "Scenery of Travels" and "Tōgenkō." <ref>"[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html Traveling with Yoshiharu Tsuge]"</ref>
== Access == * Railway **** Take the JR {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} to {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}}, then take the Ugokotsu Bus "Nyuto Line" for approximately 55 minutes to the final stop, followed by a 20-minute walk. * By car **** From {{ill|Route 341 (Japan)|ja|国道341号}}, take {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194|ja|秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. At the Nyuto Onsenkyō Holiday Village, turn right and continue 1.3 km to the parking lot near the inn.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} **** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} **** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} **** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} **** {{ill|Ōgama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} **** {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kuroyu Onsen}} * [https://kuroyu.com/ Kuroyu Onsen Official Website] * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/kuroyu.html Kuroyu Onsen] - Nyuto Onsen Association

"[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%BB%92%E6%B9%AF-1162374 黒湯[温泉]]". Revised edition, *World Encyclopedia*, Heibonsha. Retrieved November 21, 2024.</ref>

== History == The hot spring opened approximately 300 years ago, and its discovery is estimated to date back to 1674 (Enpō 2).<ref>[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html Kuroyu Onsen Official Site]</ref> It has the second-longest history in the Nyuto Onsenkyō area after {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}} and was once nicknamed "Kame no Yu" in reference to Tsurunoyu. Historically, it served as a healing retreat for the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}}.
There are legends that the name "Kuroyu" (Black Hot Spring) originated either from the dark appearance of the water or from the belief that animals would come to the spring to heal their ailments. During the Boshin War, it was used as a military outpost, with soldiers from nearby villages stationed in simple straw huts for border defense. Before the war, an elderly caretaker, known for his sharp eyes, used to hang dried snakes under the eaves.<ref>Chiba Chihei. *Furusato Hakubutsushi*, p.57.</ref>
Although Tsurunoyu is now the most popular hot spring in the Nyuto Onsenkyō, Kuroyu enjoyed the highest popularity in earlier times. On October 19, 1967, it was designated a {{ill|National Hot Spring Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of Nyuto Onsenkyō.
== Onsen Facilities == [[File:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|thumb|Wooden bathhouse with plank walls]] Kuroyu Onsen operates as a single lodging facility.
Its rustic appearance, featuring {{ill|thatch|ja|茅葺}} and cedar bark roofs, outdoor baths, and bathhouses built using locally sourced wood, is a hallmark of its charm. The facilities include three indoor baths, two outdoor baths, and two waterfalls for therapeutic bathing.
Retaining its traditional role as a {{ill|Toji|ja|湯治}} (healing retreat), Kuroyu offers both a self-catering section and a standard inn, catering to long-term visitors and climbers. Neighboring {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} is a short walk away, connected by a footbridge over the river.
Kuroyu is the only establishment in Nyuto Onsenkyō that closes during the winter, as the other six inns operate year-round.
The onsen inspired {{ill|Yoshiharu Tsuge|ja|つげ義春}}, a famous manga artist, who visited in May 1969 and September 1976. He depicted its atmosphere in four illustrations titled "Scenery of Travels" and "Tōgenkō." <ref>"[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html Traveling with Yoshiharu Tsuge]"</ref>
== Access == * Railway **** Take the JR {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} to {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}}, then take the Ugokotsu Bus "Nyuto Line" for approximately 55 minutes to the final stop, followed by a 20-minute walk. * By car **** From {{ill|Route 341 (Japan)|ja|国道341号}}, take {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194|ja|秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. At the Nyuto Onsenkyō Holiday Village, turn right and continue 1.3 km to the parking lot near the inn.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} **** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} **** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} **** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} **** {{ill|Ōgama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} **** {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}}

== Hot Spring Properties == * {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉}} Despite its name "Kuroyu" (literally "Black Hot Spring"), the water is a bluish milky white. [[File:Kuroyu Onsen 03.jpg|thumb|left|Milky white sulfur spring water]] {{Clearleft}}
== History == The hot spring opened approximately 300 years ago, and its discovery is estimated to date back to 1674 (Enpō 2).<ref>[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html Kuroyu Onsen Official Site]</ref> It has the second-longest history in the Nyuto Onsenkyō area after {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}} and was once nicknamed "Kame no Yu" in reference to Tsurunoyu. Historically, it served as a healing retreat for the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}}.
There are legends that the name "Kuroyu" (Black Hot Spring) originated either from the dark appearance of the water or from the belief that animals would come to the spring to heal their ailments. During the Boshin War, it was used as a military outpost, with soldiers from nearby villages stationed in simple straw huts for border defense. Before the war, an elderly caretaker, known for his sharp eyes, used to hang dried snakes under the eaves.<ref>Chiba Chihei. *Furusato Hakubutsushi*, p.57.</ref>
Although Tsurunoyu is now the most popular hot spring in the Nyuto Onsenkyō, Kuroyu enjoyed the highest popularity in earlier times. On October 19, 1967, it was designated a {{ill|National Hot Spring Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of Nyuto Onsenkyō.
== Onsen Facilities == [[File:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|thumb|Wooden bathhouse with plank walls]] Kuroyu Onsen operates as a single lodging facility.
Its rustic appearance, featuring {{ill|thatch|ja|茅葺}} and cedar bark roofs, outdoor baths, and bathhouses built using locally sourced wood, is a hallmark of its charm. The facilities include three indoor baths, two outdoor baths, and two waterfalls for therapeutic bathing.
Retaining its traditional role as a {{ill|Toji|ja|湯治}} (healing retreat), Kuroyu offers both a self-catering section and a standard inn, catering to long-term visitors and climbers. Neighboring {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} is a short walk away, connected by a footbridge over the river.
Kuroyu is the only establishment in Nyuto Onsenkyō that closes during the winter, as the other six inns operate year-round.
The onsen inspired {{ill|Yoshiharu Tsuge|ja|つげ義春}}, a famous manga artist, who visited in May 1969 and September 1976. He depicted its atmosphere in four illustrations titled "Scenery of Travels" and "Tōgenkō." <ref>"[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html Traveling with Yoshiharu Tsuge]"</ref>
== Access == * Railway **** Take the JR {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} to {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}}, then take the Ugokotsu Bus "Nyuto Line" for approximately 55 minutes to the final stop, followed by a 20-minute walk. * By car **** From {{ill|Route 341 (Japan)|ja|国道341号}}, take {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194|ja|秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. At the Nyuto Onsenkyō Holiday Village, turn right and continue 1.3 km to the parking lot near the inn.

{{otheruses|{{ill|Kuroyu Onsen in Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}|The black-colored hot spring in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|ja|大田区}}|Ōta’s Kuroyu Onsen}} {{otheruses|Kuroyu Onsen (Kame no Yu) in Semboku, Akita|The mineral spring "Kame no Yu" in {{ill|Hakodate, Hokkaido|ja|函館市}}|{{ill|Hakodate Mountain|ja|函館山}}#温泉}} {{Infobox hot spring | name = Kuroyu Onsen | image = 雪の黒湯温泉Img354.jpg | caption = Outdoor bath with a cedar bark roof {{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} | location = {{ill|Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|48|9|N|140|48|21|E|type:landmark_region:JP-05|display=inline,title}} | access = About 55 minutes by bus from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} (Akita Shinkansen), followed by a 20-minute walk. | hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉}} | external link = [https://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉] }}
'''Kuroyu Onsen''' (黒湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Nyuto Onsenkyō|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} in {{ill|Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}. Situated upstream of the Sendatsu River, it lies at the highest elevation within Nyuto Onsenkyō, 800 meters above sea level. Until the mid-Meiji era, it was also known as Kame no Yu.<ref>谷沢明. "[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%BB%92%E6%B9%AF-1162374 黒湯[温泉]]". Revised edition, *World Encyclopedia*, Heibonsha. Retrieved November 21, 2024.</ref>
== Hot Spring Properties == * {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉}} Despite its name "Kuroyu" (literally "Black Hot Spring"), the water is a bluish milky white. [[File:Kuroyu Onsen 03.jpg|thumb|left|Milky white sulfur spring water]] {{Clearleft}}
== History == The hot spring opened approximately 300 years ago, and its discovery is estimated to date back to 1674 (Enpō 2).<ref>[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html Kuroyu Onsen Official Site]</ref> It has the second-longest history in the Nyuto Onsenkyō area after {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}} and was once nicknamed "Kame no Yu" in reference to Tsurunoyu. Historically, it served as a healing retreat for the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}}.
There are legends that the name "Kuroyu" (Black Hot Spring) originated either from the dark appearance of the water or from the belief that animals would come to the spring to heal their ailments. During the Boshin War, it was used as a military outpost, with soldiers from nearby villages stationed in simple straw huts for border defense. Before the war, an elderly caretaker, known for his sharp eyes, used to hang dried snakes under the eaves.<ref>Chiba Chihei. *Furusato Hakubutsushi*, p.57.</ref>
Although Tsurunoyu is now the most popular hot spring in the Nyuto Onsenkyō, Kuroyu enjoyed the highest popularity in earlier times. On October 19, 1967, it was designated a {{ill|National Hot Spring Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of Nyuto Onsenkyō.
== Onsen Facilities == [[File:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|thumb|Wooden bathhouse with plank walls]] Kuroyu Onsen operates as a single lodging facility.
Its rustic appearance, featuring {{ill|thatch|ja|茅葺}} and cedar bark roofs, outdoor baths, and bathhouses built using locally sourced wood, is a hallmark of its charm. The facilities include three indoor baths, two outdoor baths, and two waterfalls for therapeutic bathing.
Retaining its traditional role as a {{ill|Toji|ja|湯治}} (healing retreat), Kuroyu offers both a self-catering section and a standard inn, catering to long-term visitors and climbers. Neighboring {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} is a short walk away, connected by a footbridge over the river.
Kuroyu is the only establishment in Nyuto Onsenkyō that closes during the winter, as the other six inns operate year-round.
The onsen inspired {{ill|Yoshiharu Tsuge|ja|つげ義春}}, a famous manga artist, who visited in May 1969 and September 1976. He depicted its atmosphere in four illustrations titled "Scenery of Travels" and "Tōgenkō." <ref>"[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html Traveling with Yoshiharu Tsuge]"</ref>
== Access == * Railway **** Take the JR {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} to {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}}, then take the Ugokotsu Bus "Nyuto Line" for approximately 55 minutes to the final stop, followed by a 20-minute walk. * By car **** From {{ill|Route 341 (Japan)|ja|国道341号}}, take {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194|ja|秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. At the Nyuto Onsenkyō Holiday Village, turn right and continue 1.3 km to the parking lot near the inn.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} **** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} **** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} **** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} **** {{ill|Ōgama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} **** {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}}

Retaining its traditional role as a {{ill|Toji|ja|湯治}} (healing retreat), Kuroyu offers both a self-catering section and a standard inn, catering to long-term visitors and climbers. Neighboring {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} is a short walk away, connected by a footbridge over the river.
Kuroyu is the only establishment in Nyuto Onsenkyō that closes during the winter, as the other six inns operate year-round.
The onsen inspired {{ill|Yoshiharu Tsuge|ja|つげ義春}}, a famous manga artist, who visited in May 1969 and September 1976. He depicted its atmosphere in four illustrations titled "Scenery of Travels" and "Tōgenkō." <ref>"[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html Traveling with Yoshiharu Tsuge]"</ref>
== Access == * Railway **** Take the JR {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} to {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}}, then take the Ugokotsu Bus "Nyuto Line" for approximately 55 minutes to the final stop, followed by a 20-minute walk. * By car **** From {{ill|Route 341 (Japan)|ja|国道341号}}, take {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194|ja|秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. At the Nyuto Onsenkyō Holiday Village, turn right and continue 1.3 km to the parking lot near the inn.

{{otheruses|{{ill|Kuroyu Onsen in Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}|The black-colored hot spring in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|ja|大田区}}|Ōta’s Kuroyu Onsen}} {{otheruses|Kuroyu Onsen (Kame no Yu) in Semboku, Akita|The mineral spring "Kame no Yu" in {{ill|Hakodate, Hokkaido|ja|函館市}}|{{ill|Hakodate Mountain|ja|函館山}}#温泉}} {{Infobox hot spring | name = Kuroyu Onsen | image = 雪の黒湯温泉Img354.jpg | caption = Outdoor bath with a cedar bark roof {{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} | location = {{ill|Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|48|9|N|140|48|21|E|type:landmark_region:JP-05|display=inline,title}} | access = About 55 minutes by bus from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} (Akita Shinkansen), followed by a 20-minute walk. | hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉}} | external link = [https://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉] }}
'''Kuroyu Onsen''' (黒湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Nyuto Onsenkyō|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} in {{ill|Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}. Situated upstream of the Sendatsu River, it lies at the highest elevation within Nyuto Onsenkyō, 800 meters above sea level. Until the mid-Meiji era, it was also known as Kame no Yu.<ref>谷沢明. "[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%BB%92%E6%B9%AF-1162374 黒湯[温泉]]". Revised edition, *World Encyclopedia*, Heibonsha. Retrieved November 21, 2024.</ref>
== Hot Spring Properties == * {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉}} Despite its name "Kuroyu" (literally "Black Hot Spring"), the water is a bluish milky white. [[File:Kuroyu Onsen 03.jpg|thumb|left|Milky white sulfur spring water]] {{Clearleft}}
== History == The hot spring opened approximately 300 years ago, and its discovery is estimated to date back to 1674 (Enpō 2).<ref>[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html Kuroyu Onsen Official Site]</ref> It has the second-longest history in the Nyuto Onsenkyō area after {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}} and was once nicknamed "Kame no Yu" in reference to Tsurunoyu. Historically, it served as a healing retreat for the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}}.
There are legends that the name "Kuroyu" (Black Hot Spring) originated either from the dark appearance of the water or from the belief that animals would come to the spring to heal their ailments. During the Boshin War, it was used as a military outpost, with soldiers from nearby villages stationed in simple straw huts for border defense. Before the war, an elderly caretaker, known for his sharp eyes, used to hang dried snakes under the eaves.<ref>Chiba Chihei. *Furusato Hakubutsushi*, p.57.</ref>
Although Tsurunoyu is now the most popular hot spring in the Nyuto Onsenkyō, Kuroyu enjoyed the highest popularity in earlier times. On October 19, 1967, it was designated a {{ill|National Hot Spring Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of Nyuto Onsenkyō.
== Onsen Facilities == [[File:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|thumb|Wooden bathhouse with plank walls]] Kuroyu Onsen operates as a single lodging facility.
Its rustic appearance, featuring {{ill|thatch|ja|茅葺}} and cedar bark roofs, outdoor baths, and bathhouses built using locally sourced wood, is a hallmark of its charm. The facilities include three indoor baths, two outdoor baths, and two waterfalls for therapeutic bathing.
Retaining its traditional role as a {{ill|Toji|ja|湯治}} (healing retreat), Kuroyu offers both a self-catering section and a standard inn, catering to long-term visitors and climbers. Neighboring {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} is a short walk away, connected by a footbridge over the river.
Kuroyu is the only establishment in Nyuto Onsenkyō that closes during the winter, as the other six inns operate year-round.
The onsen inspired {{ill|Yoshiharu Tsuge|ja|つげ義春}}, a famous manga artist, who visited in May 1969 and September 1976. He depicted its atmosphere in four illustrations titled "Scenery of Travels" and "Tōgenkō." <ref>"[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html Traveling with Yoshiharu Tsuge]"</ref>
== Access == * Railway **** Take the JR {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} to {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}}, then take the Ugokotsu Bus "Nyuto Line" for approximately 55 minutes to the final stop, followed by a 20-minute walk. * By car **** From {{ill|Route 341 (Japan)|ja|国道341号}}, take {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194|ja|秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. At the Nyuto Onsenkyō Holiday Village, turn right and continue 1.3 km to the parking lot near the inn.

{{otheruses|{{ill|Kuroyu Onsen in Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}|The black-colored hot spring in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|ja|大田区}}|Ōta’s Kuroyu Onsen}} {{otheruses|Kuroyu Onsen (Kame no Yu) in Semboku, Akita|The mineral spring "Kame no Yu" in {{ill|Hakodate, Hokkaido|ja|函館市}}|{{ill|Hakodate Mountain|ja|函館山}}#温泉}} {{Infobox hot spring | name = Kuroyu Onsen | image = 雪の黒湯温泉Img354.jpg | caption = Outdoor bath with a cedar bark roof {{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} | location = {{ill|Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|48|9|N|140|48|21|E|type:landmark_region:JP-05|display=inline,title}} | access = About 55 minutes by bus from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} (Akita Shinkansen), followed by a 20-minute walk. | hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉}} | external link = [https://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉] }}
'''Kuroyu Onsen''' (黒湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Nyuto Onsenkyō|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} in {{ill|Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}. Situated upstream of the Sendatsu River, it lies at the highest elevation within Nyuto Onsenkyō, 800 meters above sea level. Until the mid-Meiji era, it was also known as Kame no Yu.<ref>谷沢明. "[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%BB%92%E6%B9%AF-1162374 黒湯[温泉]]". Revised edition, *World Encyclopedia*, Heibonsha. Retrieved November 21, 2024.</ref>
== Hot Spring Properties == * {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉}} Despite its name "Kuroyu" (literally "Black Hot Spring"), the water is a bluish milky white. [[File:Kuroyu Onsen 03.jpg|thumb|left|Milky white sulfur spring water]] {{Clearleft}}
== History == The hot spring opened approximately 300 years ago, and its discovery is estimated to date back to 1674 (Enpō 2).<ref>[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html Kuroyu Onsen Official Site]</ref> It has the second-longest history in the Nyuto Onsenkyō area after {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}} and was once nicknamed "Kame no Yu" in reference to Tsurunoyu. Historically, it served as a healing retreat for the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}}.
There are legends that the name "Kuroyu" (Black Hot Spring) originated either from the dark appearance of the water or from the belief that animals would come to the spring to heal their ailments. During the Boshin War, it was used as a military outpost, with soldiers from nearby villages stationed in simple straw huts for border defense. Before the war, an elderly caretaker, known for his sharp eyes, used to hang dried snakes under the eaves.<ref>Chiba Chihei. *Furusato Hakubutsushi*, p.57.</ref>
Although Tsurunoyu is now the most popular hot spring in the Nyuto Onsenkyō, Kuroyu enjoyed the highest popularity in earlier times. On October 19, 1967, it was designated a {{ill|National Hot Spring Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of Nyuto Onsenkyō.
== Onsen Facilities == [[File:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|thumb|Wooden bathhouse with plank walls]] Kuroyu Onsen operates as a single lodging facility.
Its rustic appearance, featuring {{ill|thatch|ja|茅葺}} and cedar bark roofs, outdoor baths, and bathhouses built using locally sourced wood, is a hallmark of its charm. The facilities include three indoor baths, two outdoor baths, and two waterfalls for therapeutic bathing.
Retaining its traditional role as a {{ill|Toji|ja|湯治}} (healing retreat), Kuroyu offers both a self-catering section and a standard inn, catering to long-term visitors and climbers. Neighboring {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} is a short walk away, connected by a footbridge over the river.
Kuroyu is the only establishment in Nyuto Onsenkyō that closes during the winter, as the other six inns operate year-round.
The onsen inspired {{ill|Yoshiharu Tsuge|ja|つげ義春}}, a famous manga artist, who visited in May 1969 and September 1976. He depicted its atmosphere in four illustrations titled "Scenery of Travels" and "Tōgenkō." <ref>"[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html Traveling with Yoshiharu Tsuge]"</ref>
== Access == * Railway **** Take the JR {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} to {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}}, then take the Ugokotsu Bus "Nyuto Line" for approximately 55 minutes to the final stop, followed by a 20-minute walk. * By car **** From {{ill|Route 341 (Japan)|ja|国道341号}}, take {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194|ja|秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. At the Nyuto Onsenkyō Holiday Village, turn right and continue 1.3 km to the parking lot near the inn.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} **** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} **** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} **** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} **** {{ill|Ōgama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} **** {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kuroyu Onsen}} * [https://kuroyu.com/ Kuroyu Onsen Official Website] * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/kuroyu.html Kuroyu Onsen] - Nyuto Onsen Association

Although Tsurunoyu is now the most popular hot spring in the Nyuto Onsenkyō, Kuroyu enjoyed the highest popularity in earlier times. On October 19, 1967, it was designated a {{ill|National Hot Spring Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of Nyuto Onsenkyō.
== Onsen Facilities == [[File:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|thumb|Wooden bathhouse with plank walls]] Kuroyu Onsen operates as a single lodging facility.
Its rustic appearance, featuring {{ill|thatch|ja|茅葺}} and cedar bark roofs, outdoor baths, and bathhouses built using locally sourced wood, is a hallmark of its charm. The facilities include three indoor baths, two outdoor baths, and two waterfalls for therapeutic bathing.
Retaining its traditional role as a {{ill|Toji|ja|湯治}} (healing retreat), Kuroyu offers both a self-catering section and a standard inn, catering to long-term visitors and climbers. Neighboring {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} is a short walk away, connected by a footbridge over the river.
Kuroyu is the only establishment in Nyuto Onsenkyō that closes during the winter, as the other six inns operate year-round.
The onsen inspired {{ill|Yoshiharu Tsuge|ja|つげ義春}}, a famous manga artist, who visited in May 1969 and September 1976. He depicted its atmosphere in four illustrations titled "Scenery of Travels" and "Tōgenkō." <ref>"[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html Traveling with Yoshiharu Tsuge]"</ref>

At the Nyuto Onsenkyō Holiday Village, turn right and continue 1.3 km to the parking lot near the inn.

'''Kuroyu Onsen''' (黒湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Nyuto Onsenkyō|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} in {{ill|Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}. Situated upstream of the Sendatsu River, it lies at the highest elevation within Nyuto Onsenkyō, 800 meters above sea level. Until the mid-Meiji era, it was also known as Kame no Yu.<ref>谷沢明. "[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%BB%92%E6%B9%AF-1162374 黒湯[温泉]]". Revised edition, *World Encyclopedia*, Heibonsha. Retrieved November 21, 2024.</ref>
== Hot Spring Properties == * {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉}} Despite its name "Kuroyu" (literally "Black Hot Spring"), the water is a bluish milky white. [[File:Kuroyu Onsen 03.jpg|thumb|left|Milky white sulfur spring water]] {{Clearleft}}
== History == The hot spring opened approximately 300 years ago, and its discovery is estimated to date back to 1674 (Enpō 2).<ref>[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html Kuroyu Onsen Official Site]</ref> It has the second-longest history in the Nyuto Onsenkyō area after {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}} and was once nicknamed "Kame no Yu" in reference to Tsurunoyu. Historically, it served as a healing retreat for the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}}.
There are legends that the name "Kuroyu" (Black Hot Spring) originated either from the dark appearance of the water or from the belief that animals would come to the spring to heal their ailments. During the Boshin War, it was used as a military outpost, with soldiers from nearby villages stationed in simple straw huts for border defense. Before the war, an elderly caretaker, known for his sharp eyes, used to hang dried snakes under the eaves.<ref>Chiba Chihei. *Furusato Hakubutsushi*, p.57.</ref>
Although Tsurunoyu is now the most popular hot spring in the Nyuto Onsenkyō, Kuroyu enjoyed the highest popularity in earlier times. On October 19, 1967, it was designated a {{ill|National Hot Spring Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of Nyuto Onsenkyō.

{{otheruses|{{ill|Kuroyu Onsen in Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}|The black-colored hot spring in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|ja|大田区}}|Ōta’s Kuroyu Onsen}} {{otheruses|Kuroyu Onsen (Kame no Yu) in Semboku, Akita|The mineral spring "Kame no Yu" in {{ill|Hakodate, Hokkaido|ja|函館市}}|{{ill|Hakodate Mountain|ja|函館山}}#温泉}} {{Infobox hot spring | name = Kuroyu Onsen | image = 雪の黒湯温泉Img354.jpg | caption = Outdoor bath with a cedar bark roof {{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} | location = {{ill|Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|48|9|N|140|48|21|E|type:landmark_region:JP-05|display=inline,title}} | access = About 55 minutes by bus from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} (Akita Shinkansen), followed by a 20-minute walk. | hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|1=Sulfur spring}} | external link = [https://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉] }}
'''Kuroyu Onsen''' (黒湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Nyuto Onsenkyō|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} in {{ill|Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}. Situated upstream of the Sendatsu River, it lies at the highest elevation within Nyuto Onsenkyō, 800 meters above sea level. Until the mid-Meiji era, it was also known as Kame no Yu.<ref>谷沢明. "[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%BB%92%E6%B9%AF-1162374 黒湯[温泉]]". Revised edition, *World Encyclopedia*, Heibonsha. Retrieved November 21, 2024.</ref>
== Hot Spring Properties == * {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|1=Sulfur spring}} Despite its name "Kuroyu" (literally "Black Hot Spring"), the water is a bluish milky white. [[File:Kuroyu Onsen 03.jpg|thumb|left|Milky white sulfur spring water]] {{Clearleft}}
== History == The hot spring opened approximately 300 years ago, and its discovery is estimated to date back to 1674 (Enpō 2).<ref>[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html Kuroyu Onsen Official Site]</ref> It has the second-longest history in the Nyuto Onsenkyō area after {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}} and was once nicknamed "Kame no Yu" in reference to Tsurunoyu. Historically, it served as a healing retreat for the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}}.
There are legends that the name "Kuroyu" (Black Hot Spring) originated either from the dark appearance of the water or from the belief that animals would come to the spring to heal their ailments. During the Boshin War, it was used as a military outpost, with soldiers from nearby villages stationed in simple straw huts for border defense. Before the war, an elderly caretaker, known for his sharp eyes, used to hang dried snakes under the eaves.<ref>Chiba Chihei. *Furusato Hakubutsushi*, p.57.</ref>
Although Tsurunoyu is now the most popular hot spring in the Nyuto Onsenkyō, Kuroyu enjoyed the highest popularity in earlier times. On October 19, 1967, it was designated a {{ill|National Hot Spring Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of Nyuto Onsenkyō.
== Onsen Facilities == [[File:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|thumb|Wooden bathhouse with plank walls]] Kuroyu Onsen operates as a single lodging facility.
Its rustic appearance, featuring {{ill|thatch|ja|茅葺}} and cedar bark roofs, outdoor baths, and bathhouses built using locally sourced wood, is a hallmark of its charm. The facilities include three indoor baths, two outdoor baths, and two waterfalls for therapeutic bathing.
Retaining its traditional role as a {{ill|Toji|ja|湯治}} (healing retreat), Kuroyu offers both a self-catering section and a standard inn, catering to long-term visitors and climbers. Neighboring {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} is a short walk away, connected by a footbridge over the river.
Kuroyu is the only establishment in Nyuto Onsenkyō that closes during the winter, as the other six inns operate year-round.
The onsen inspired {{ill|Yoshiharu Tsuge|ja|つげ義春}}, a famous manga artist, who visited in May 1969 and September 1976. He depicted its atmosphere in four illustrations titled "Scenery of Travels" and "Tōgenkō." <ref>"[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html Traveling with Yoshiharu Tsuge]"</ref>
== Access == * Railway **** Take the JR {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} to {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}}, then take the Ugokotsu Bus "Nyuto Line" for approximately 55 minutes to the final stop, followed by a 20-minute walk. * By car **** From {{ill|Route 341 (Japan)|ja|国道341号}}, take {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194|ja|秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. At the Nyuto Onsenkyō Holiday Village, turn right and continue 1.3 km to the parking lot near the inn.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} **** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} **** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} **** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} **** {{ill|Ōgama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} **** {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kuroyu Onsen}} * [https://kuroyu.com/ Kuroyu Onsen Official Website] * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/kuroyu.html Kuroyu Onsen] - Nyuto Onsen Association
{{Hot springs in Japan}}

At the Nyuto Onsenkyō Holiday Village, turn right and continue 1.3 km to the parking lot near the inn.

{{otheruses|{{ill|Kuroyu Onsen in Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}|The black-colored hot spring in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|ja|大田区}}|Ōta’s Kuroyu Onsen}} {{otheruses|Kuroyu Onsen (Kame no Yu) in Semboku, Akita|The mineral spring "Kame no Yu" in {{ill|Hakodate, Hokkaido|ja|函館市}}|{{ill|Hakodate Mountain|ja|函館山}}#温泉}} {{Infobox hot spring | name = Kuroyu Onsen | image = 雪の黒湯温泉Img354.jpg | caption = Outdoor bath with a cedar bark roof {{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} | location = {{ill|Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|48|9|N|140|48|21|E|type:landmark_region:JP-05|display=inline,title}} | access = About 55 minutes by bus from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} (Akita Shinkansen), followed by a 20-minute walk. | hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|1=Sulfur spring}} | external link = [https://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉] }}
'''Kuroyu Onsen''' (黒湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Nyuto Onsenkyō|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} in {{ill|Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}. Situated upstream of the Sendatsu River, it lies at the highest elevation within Nyuto Onsenkyō, 800 meters above sea level. Until the mid-Meiji era, it was also known as Kame no Yu.<ref>谷沢明. "[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%BB%92%E6%B9%AF-qq62374 黒湯[温泉]]". Revised edition, *World Encyclopedia*, Heibonsha. Retrieved November 21, 2024.</ref>
== Hot Spring Properties == * {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|1=Sulfur spring}} Despite its name "Kuroyu" (literally "Black Hot Spring"), the water is a bluish milky white. [[File:Kuroyu Onsen 03.jpg|thumb|left|Milky white sulfur spring water]] {{Clearleft}}
== History == The hot spring opened approximately 300 years ago, and its discovery is estimated to date back to 1674 (Enpō 2).<ref>[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html Kuroyu Onsen Official Site]</ref> It has the second-longest history in the Nyuto Onsenkyō area after {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}} and was once nicknamed "Kame no Yu" in reference to Tsurunoyu. Historically, it served as a healing retreat for the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}}.
There are legends that the name "Kuroyu" (Black Hot Spring) originated either from the dark appearance of the water or from the belief that animals would come to the spring to heal their ailments. During the Boshin War, it was used as a military outpost, with soldiers from nearby villages stationed in simple straw huts for border defense. Before the war, an elderly caretaker, known for his sharp eyes, used to hang dried snakes under the eaves.<ref>Chiba Chihei. *Furusato Hakubutsushi*, p.57.</ref>
Although Tsurunoyu is now the most popular hot spring in the Nyuto Onsenkyō, Kuroyu enjoyed the highest popularity in earlier times. On October 19, 1967, it was designated a {{ill|National Hot Spring Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of Nyuto Onsenkyō.
== Onsen Facilities == [[File:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|thumb|Wooden bathhouse with plank walls]] Kuroyu Onsen operates as a single lodging facility.
Its rustic appearance, featuring {{ill|thatch|ja|茅葺}} and cedar bark roofs, outdoor baths, and bathhouses built using locally sourced wood, is a hallmark of its charm. The facilities include three indoor baths, two outdoor baths, and two waterfalls for therapeutic bathing.
Retaining its traditional role as a {{ill|Toji|ja|湯治}} (healing retreat), Kuroyu offers both a self-catering section and a standard inn, catering to long-term visitors and climbers. Neighboring {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} is a short walk away, connected by a footbridge over the river.
Kuroyu is the only establishment in Nyuto Onsenkyō that closes during the winter, as the other six inns operate year-round.
The onsen inspired {{ill|Yoshiharu Tsuge|ja|つげ義春}}, a famous manga artist, who visited in May 1969 and September 1976. He depicted its atmosphere in four illustrations titled "Scenery of Travels" and "Tōgenkō." <ref>"[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html Traveling with Yoshiharu Tsuge]"</ref>
== Access == * Railway **** Take the JR {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} to {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}}, then take the Ugokotsu Bus "Nyuto Line" for approximately 55 minutes to the final stop, followed by a 20-minute walk. * By car **** From {{ill|Route 341 (Japan)|ja|国道341号}}, take {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194|ja|秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. At the Nyuto Onsenkyō Holiday Village, turn right and continue 1.3 km to the parking lot near the inn.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} **** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} **** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} **** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} **** {{ill|Ōgama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} **** {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}}

Historically, it served as a healing retreat for the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}}.
There are legends that the name "Kuroyu" (Black Hot Spring) originated either from the dark appearance of the water or from the belief that animals would come to the spring to heal their ailments. During the Boshin War, it was used as a military outpost, with soldiers from nearby villages stationed in simple straw huts for border defense. {{otheruses|{{ill|Kuroyu Onsen in Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}|The black-colored hot spring in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|ja|大田区}}|Ōta’s Kuroyu Onsen}} {{otheruses|Kuroyu Onsen (Kame no Yu) in Semboku, Akita|The mineral spring "Kame no Yu" in {{ill|Hakodate, Hokkaido|ja|函館市}}|{{ill|Hakodate Mountain|ja|函館山}}#温泉}} {{Infobox hot spring | name = Kuroyu Onsen | image = 雪の黒湯温泉Img354.jpg | caption = Outdoor bath with a cedar bark roof {{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} | location = {{ill|Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|48|9|N|140|48|21|E|type:landmark_region:JP-05|display=inline,title}} | access = About 55 minutes by bus from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} (Akita Shinkansen), followed by a 20-minute walk. | hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|1=Sulfur spring}} | external link = [https://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉] }}
'''Kuroyu Onsen''' (黒湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Nyuto Onsenkyō|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} in {{ill|Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}. Situated upstream of the Sendatsu River, it lies at the highest elevation within Nyuto Onsenkyō, 800 meters above sea level. Until the mid-Meiji era, it was also known as Kame no Yu.<ref>谷沢明. "[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%BB%92%E6%B9%AF-1162374 黒湯[温泉]]". Revised edition, *World Encyclopedia*, Heibonsha. Retrieved November 21, 2024.</ref>
== Hot Spring Properties == * {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|1=Sulfur spring}} Despite its name "Kuroyu" (literally "Black Hot Spring"), the water is a bluish milky white. [[File:Kuroyu Onsen 03.jpg|thumb|left|Milky white sulfur spring water]] {{Clearleft}}
== History == The hot spring opened approximately 300 years ago, and its discovery is estimated to date back to 1674 (Enpō 2).<ref>[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html Kuroyu Onsen Official Site]</ref> It has the second-longest history in the Nyuto Onsenkyō area after {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}} and was once nicknamed "Kame no Yu" in reference to Tsurunoyu. Historically, it served as a healing retreat for the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}}.
There are legends that the name "Kuroyu" (Black Hot Spring) originated either from the dark appearance of the water or from the belief that animals would come to the spring to heal their ailments. During the Boshin War, it was used as a military outpost, with soldiers from nearby villages stationed in simple straw huts for border defense. Before the war, an elderly caretaker, known for his sharp eyes, used to hang dried snakes under the eaves.<ref>Chiba Chihei. *Furusato Hakubutsushi*, p.57.</ref>
Although Tsurunoyu is now the most popular hot spring in the Nyuto Onsenkyō, Kuroyu enjoyed the highest popularity in earlier times. On October 19, 1967, it was designated a {{ill|National Hot Spring Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of Nyuto Onsenkyō.
== Onsen Facilities == [[File:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|thumb|Wooden bathhouse with plank walls]] Kuroyu Onsen operates as a single lodging facility.
Its rustic appearance, featuring {{ill|thatch|ja|茅葺}} and cedar bark roofs, outdoor baths, and bathhouses built using locally sourced wood, is a hallmark of its charm. The facilities include three indoor baths, two outdoor baths, and two waterfalls for therapeutic bathing.
Retaining its traditional role as a {{ill|Toji|ja|湯治}} (healing retreat), Kuroyu offers both a self-catering section and a standard inn, catering to long-term visitors and climbers. Neighboring {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} is a short walk away, connected by a footbridge over the river.
Kuroyu is the only establishment in Nyuto Onsenkyō that closes during the winter, as the other six inns operate year-round.
The onsen inspired {{ill|Yoshiharu Tsuge|ja|つげ義春}}, a famous manga artist, who visited in May 1969 and September 1976. He depicted its atmosphere in four illustrations titled "Scenery of Travels" and "Tōgenkō." <ref>"[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html Traveling with Yoshiharu Tsuge]"</ref>
== Access == * Railway **** Take the JR {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} to {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}}, then take the Ugokotsu Bus "Nyuto Line" for approximately 55 minutes to the final stop, followed by a 20-minute walk. * By car **** From {{ill|Route 341 (Japan)|ja|国道341号}}, take {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194|ja|秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. At the Nyuto Onsenkyō Holiday Village, turn right and continue 1.3 km to the parking lot near the inn.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} **** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} **** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} **** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} **** {{ill|Ōgama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} **** {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}}

Its rustic appearance, featuring {{ill|thatch|ja|茅葺}} and cedar bark roofs, outdoor baths, and bathhouses built using locally sourced wood, is a hallmark of its charm. The facilities include three indoor baths, two outdoor baths, and two waterfalls for therapeutic bathing.
Retaining its traditional role as a {{ill|Toji|ja|湯治}} (healing retreat), Kuroyu offers both a self-catering section and a standard inn, catering to long-term visitors and climbers. Neighboring {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} is a short walk away, connected by a footbridge over the river.
Kuroyu is the only establishment in Nyuto Onsenkyō that closes during the winter, as the other six inns operate year-round.
The onsen inspired {{ill|Yoshiharu Tsuge|ja|つげ義春}}, a famous manga artist, who visited in May 1969 and September 1976. He depicted its atmosphere in four illustrations titled "Scenery of Travels" and "Tōgenkō." <ref>"[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html Traveling with Yoshiharu Tsuge]"</ref>
== Access == * Railway **** Take the JR {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} to {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}}, then take the Ugokotsu Bus "Nyuto Line" for approximately 55 minutes to the final stop, followed by a 20-minute walk. * By car **** From {{ill|Route 341 (Japan)|ja|国道341号}}, take {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194|ja|秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. At the Nyuto Onsenkyō Holiday Village, turn right and continue 1.3 km to the parking lot near the inn.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} **** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} **** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} **** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} **** {{ill|Ōgama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} **** {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kuroyu Onsen}} * [https://kuroyu.com/ Kuroyu Onsen Official Website] * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/kuroyu.html Kuroyu Onsen] - Nyuto Onsen Association

revision.text:
{{otheruses|{{ill|Kuroyu Onsen in Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}|The black-colored hot spring in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|ja|大田区}}|Ōta’s Kuroyu Onsen}} {{otheruses|Kuroyu Onsen (Kame no Yu) in Semboku, Akita|The mineral spring "Kame no Yu" in {{ill|Hakodate, Hokkaido|ja|函館市}}|{{ill|Hakodate Mountain|ja|函館山}}#温泉}} {{Infobox hot spring | name = Kuroyu Onsen | image = 雪の黒湯温泉Img354.jpg | caption = Outdoor bath with a cedar bark roof {{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} | location = {{ill|Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|48|9|N|140|48|21|E|type:landmark_region:JP-05|display=inline,title}} | access = About 55 minutes by bus from {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}} (Akita Shinkansen), followed by a 20-minute walk. | hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|1=Sulfur spring}} | external link = [https://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉] }}
'''Kuroyu Onsen''' (黒湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Nyuto Onsenkyō|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} in {{ill|Semboku, Akita|ja|仙北市}}. Situated upstream of the Sendatsu River, it lies at the highest elevation within Nyuto Onsenkyō, 800 meters above sea level. Until the mid-Meiji era, it was also known as Kame no Yu.<ref>谷沢明. "[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%BB%92%E6%B9%AF-1162374 黒湯[温泉]]". Revised edition, *World Encyclopedia*, Heibonsha. Retrieved November 21, 2024.</ref>
== Hot Spring Properties == * {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|1=Sulfur spring}} Despite its name "Kuroyu" (literally "Black Hot Spring"), the water is a bluish milky white. [[File:Kuroyu Onsen 03.jpg|thumb|left|Milky white sulfur spring water]] {{Clearleft}}
== History == The hot spring opened approximately 300 years ago, and its discovery is estimated to date back to 1674 (Enpō 2).<ref>[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html Kuroyu Onsen Official Site]</ref> It has the second-longest history in the Nyuto Onsenkyō area after {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}} and was once nicknamed "Kame no Yu" in reference to Tsurunoyu. Historically, it served as a healing retreat for the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩}}.
There are legends that the name "Kuroyu" (Black Hot Spring) originated either from the dark appearance of the water or from the belief that animals would come to the spring to heal their ailments. During the Boshin War, it was used as a military outpost, with soldiers from nearby villages stationed in simple straw huts for border defense. Before the war, an elderly caretaker, known for his sharp eyes, used to hang dried snakes under the eaves.<ref>Chiba Chihei. *Furusato Hakubutsushi*, p.57.</ref>
Although Tsurunoyu is now the most popular hot spring in the Nyuto Onsenkyō, Kuroyu enjoyed the highest popularity in earlier times. On October 19, 1967, it was designated a {{ill|National Hot Spring Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} as part of Nyuto Onsenkyō.
== Onsen Facilities == [[File:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|thumb|Wooden bathhouse with plank walls]] Kuroyu Onsen operates as a single lodging facility.
Its rustic appearance, featuring {{ill|thatch|ja|茅葺}} and cedar bark roofs, outdoor baths, and bathhouses built using locally sourced wood, is a hallmark of its charm. The facilities include three indoor baths, two outdoor baths, and two waterfalls for therapeutic bathing.
Retaining its traditional role as a {{ill|Toji|ja|湯治}} (healing retreat), Kuroyu offers both a self-catering section and a standard inn, catering to long-term visitors and climbers. Neighboring {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} is a short walk away, connected by a footbridge over the river.
Kuroyu is the only establishment in Nyuto Onsenkyō that closes during the winter, as the other six inns operate year-round.
The onsen inspired {{ill|Yoshiharu Tsuge|ja|つげ義春}}, a famous manga artist, who visited in May 1969 and September 1976. He depicted its atmosphere in four illustrations titled "Scenery of Travels" and "Tōgenkō." <ref>"[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html Traveling with Yoshiharu Tsuge]"</ref>
== Access == * Railway **** Take the JR {{ill|Akita Shinkansen|ja|秋田新幹線}} to {{ill|Tazawako Station|ja|田沢湖駅}}, then take the Ugokotsu Bus "Nyuto Line" for approximately 55 minutes to the final stop, followed by a 20-minute walk. * By car **** From {{ill|Route 341 (Japan)|ja|国道341号}}, take {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194|ja|秋田県道194号西山生保内線}}. At the Nyuto Onsenkyō Holiday Village, turn right and continue 1.3 km to the parking lot near the inn.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Nyūtō Onsen|ja|乳頭温泉郷}} **** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉}} **** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉}} **** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉}} **** {{ill|Ōgama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉}} **** {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉}}

revision.text:
{{otheruses|[[秋田県]][[仙北市]]の黒湯温泉|東京都大田区にある黒い色の温泉|大田区の黒湯温泉}} {{otheruses|秋田県仙北市の黒湯温泉（亀ノ湯）|[[北海道]][[函館市]]にある鉱泉「亀ノ湯」|[[函館山#温泉]]}} {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 黒湯温泉 |画像 = 雪の黒湯温泉Img354.jpg |コメント = 杉皮葺屋根の外風呂{{Infobox mapframe|frame-width=300|zoom=9}} |所在地 = [[秋田県]][[仙北市]] |座標 = {{ウィキ座標度分秒|39|48|9|N|140|48|21|E|type:landmark_region:JP-05|display=inline,title}} |交通アクセス = [[秋田新幹線]][[田沢湖駅]]よりバスで約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分 |泉質 = [[硫黄泉]] |泉温 = |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = [https://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉] |特記事項 = }} '''黒湯温泉'''（くろゆおんせん）は、[[秋田県]][[仙北市]]の[[乳頭温泉郷]]にある[[温泉]]。温泉郷の最奥部先達川上流に位置する。乳頭温泉郷で一番高い標高800m地点にある。明治中期までは亀ノ湯ともよばれた<ref>谷沢明 [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%BB%92%E6%B9%AF-1162374 "黒湯[温泉<nowiki>]</nowiki>"] 改訂新版 世界大百科事典 平凡社 2024年11月21日閲覧</ref>。
== 泉質 == * [[硫黄泉]] 温泉名は[[黒湯]]だが、温泉の色は青みがかった乳白色である。 [[ファイル:Kuroyu Onsen 03.jpg|thumb|left|湯は乳白色の[[硫黄泉]]]] {{Clearleft}} == 歴史 == 開湯は約300年前。発見は[[1674年]]（[[延宝]]2年）頃と推測される<ref>[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html 黒湯温泉] （公式サイト）</ref>。温泉郷内では[[鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)|鶴の湯温泉]]に次ぐ歴史があり、鶴の湯に因んで亀の湯の別称があった。古くは[[秋田藩]]の湯治場であった。
獣が病気を癒しに来ることから、諸獣の來留湯(くるゆ)と名付けたという言い伝えや、湯が黒みがかって見えるから黒湯と言ったなどの言い伝えがある。戊辰戦争では国境警備の最前線として、田沢村の農兵や横手の兵隊が藁葺き小屋に泊まって張り番をした。戦前は蛇捕りを副業としている目つきの鋭い老人が湯番をしていて、干した蛇を軒に沢山吊していた<ref>千葉治平『ふるさと博物誌』p.57</ref>。
現在の乳頭温泉郷は[[鶴の湯]]が最も人気があるが、以前は黒湯が一番人気を誇っていた。[[1967年]]（昭和42年）[[10月19日]]、乳頭温泉郷の一部として[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定された。
== 温泉地 == [[ファイル:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|thumb|木造板張りの湯小屋]] 一軒宿の「黒湯温泉」が営業する。
[[茅葺]]、杉皮葺屋根、周囲の[[雑木林]]から伐りだした木材をそのまま利用した[[露天風呂]]、湯小屋など、鄙びた外観が特徴である。[[内湯]]を3つ、露天風呂を2つ、[[打たせ湯]]を2つ有する。
古くから[[湯治#湯治場|湯治場]]であった伝統を色濃く残し、[[秘湯]]ブーム以降、観光地化した現在でも旅館部の他に自炊部を置く。湯治客のほか、古くから登山客の利用も多い。同じく一軒宿の[[孫六温泉]]とは、川にかかった人道橋を徒歩で渡ってすぐのところにある。
乳頭温泉郷の旅館の中で唯一、冬季は休業している（ほかの6館は通年営業）。
なお[[漫画家]]の[[つげ義春]]は、[[昭和40年代]]（1969年5月、1976年9月）に訪れ、当時の湯治場の雰囲気を4枚の[[イラスト]]にして残している（「旅の景色」、「桃源行」）<ref>“[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html つげ義春を旅する]”</ref>。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 ** [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[秋田新幹線]]・[[田沢湖線]] [[田沢湖駅]]より[[羽後交通]]の路線バス「乳頭線」で約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分。 * 車 ** [[国道341号]]から[[秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線]]・[[秋田県道194号西山生保内線]]を経由し、休暇村乳頭温泉郷の前から右手に分け入り、1.3kmほど進むと駐車場がある。駐車場の下に宿がある。
== 周辺 == * [[乳頭温泉郷]] ** [[孫六温泉]] ** [[一本松温泉]] ** [[蟹場温泉]] ** [[大釜温泉]] ** [[妙乃湯温泉]] ** [[鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)|鶴の湯温泉]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kuroyu Onsen}} * [https://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉公式ホームページ] * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/kuroyu.html 黒湯温泉] - 乳頭温泉組合
{{温泉}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くろゆおんせん}} [[Category:秋田県の温泉]] [[Category:国民保養温泉地]] [[Category:仙北市の地理]]

== 温泉地 == [[ファイル:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|thumb|木造板張りの湯小屋]] 一軒宿の「黒湯温泉」が営業する。
[[茅葺]]、杉皮葺屋根、周囲の[[雑木林]]から伐りだした木材をそのまま利用した[[露天風呂]]、湯小屋など、鄙びた外観が特徴である。[[内湯]]を3つ、露天風呂を2つ、[[打たせ湯]]を2つ有する。
古くから[[湯治#湯治場|湯治場]]であった伝統を色濃く残し、[[秘湯]]ブーム以降、観光地化した現在でも旅館部の他に自炊部を置く。湯治客のほか、古くから登山客の利用も多い。同じく一軒宿の[[孫六温泉]]とは、川にかかった人道橋を徒歩で渡ってすぐのところにある。
乳頭温泉郷の旅館の中で唯一、冬季は休業している（ほかの6館は通年営業）。
なお[[漫画家]]の[[つげ義春]]は、[[昭和40年代]]（1969年5月、1976年9月）に訪れ、当時の湯治場の雰囲気を4枚の[[イラスト]]にして残している（「旅の景色」、「桃源行」）<ref>“[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html つげ義春を旅する]”</ref>。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 ** [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[秋田新幹線]]・[[田沢湖線]] [[田沢湖駅]]より[[羽後交通]]の路線バス「乳頭線」で約55分で終点。そこから徒歩約20分。 * 車 ** [[国道341号]]から[[秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線]]・[[秋田県道194号西山生保内線]]を経由し、休暇村乳頭温泉郷の前から右手に分け入り、1.3kmほど進むと駐車場がある。駐車場の下に宿がある。
== 周辺 == * [[乳頭温泉郷]] ** [[孫六温泉]] ** [[一本松温泉]] ** [[蟹場温泉]] ** [[大釜温泉]] ** [[妙乃湯温泉]] ** [[鶴の湯温泉 (秋田県)|鶴の湯温泉]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kuroyu Onsen}} * [https://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉公式ホームページ] * [http://www.nyuto-onsenkyo.com/kuroyu.html 黒湯温泉] - 乳頭温泉組合
{{温泉}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くろゆおんせん}} [[Category:秋田県の温泉]] [[Category:国民保養温泉地]] [[Category:仙北市の地理]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11678461]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}

| hot spring type = {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|1=Sulfur spring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfur spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfur spring|qq=}}
| external link = [https://kuroyu.com/ 黒湯温泉]
}}

'''Kuroyu Onsen''' (黒湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyuto Onsenkyō]] in [[:en:Semboku, Akita|Semboku, Akita]]. Situated upstream of the Sendatsu River, it lies at the highest elevation within Nyuto Onsenkyō, 800 meters above sea level. Until the mid-Meiji era, it was also known as Kame no Yu.<ref>谷沢明. "[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%BB%92%E6%B9%AF-1162374 黒湯[温泉]]". Revised edition, *World Encyclopedia*, Heibonsha. Retrieved November 21, 2024.</ref>

== Hot Spring Properties ==
* {{ill|Sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|1=Sulfur spring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfur spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfur spring|qq=}}
Despite its name "Kuroyu" (literally "Black Hot Spring"), the water is a bluish milky white.
[[File:Kuroyu Onsen 03.jpg|thumb|left|Milky white sulfur spring water]]
{{Clearleft}}

== History ==
The hot spring opened approximately 300 years ago, and its discovery is estimated to date back to 1674 (Enpō 2).<ref>[http://kuroyu.com/kuroyu/kuroyu.html Kuroyu Onsen Official Site]</ref> It has the second-longest history in the Nyuto Onsenkyō area after {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen|qq=}} and was once nicknamed "Kame no Yu" in reference to Tsurunoyu. Historically, it served as a healing retreat for the {{ill|Akita Domain|ja|秋田藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久保田藩|1=Kubota Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067720}}.

There are legends that the name "Kuroyu" (Black Hot Spring) originated either from the dark appearance of the water or from the belief that animals would come to the spring to heal their ailments. During the Boshin War, it was used as a military outpost, with soldiers from nearby villages stationed in simple straw huts for border defense. Before the war, an elderly caretaker, known for his sharp eyes, used to hang dried snakes under the eaves.<ref>Chiba Chihei. *Furusato Hakubutsushi*, p.57.</ref>

Although Tsurunoyu is now the most popular hot spring in the Nyuto Onsenkyō, Kuroyu enjoyed the highest popularity in earlier times. On October 19, 1967, it was designated a {{ill|National Hot Spring Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Hot Spring Health Resort|qq=|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} as part of Nyuto Onsenkyō.

== Onsen Facilities ==
[[File:雪の黒湯温泉Img360.jpg|thumb|Wooden bathhouse with plank walls]]
Kuroyu Onsen operates as a single lodging facility.

Its rustic appearance, featuring [[:en:Thatching|thatch]] and cedar bark roofs, outdoor baths, and bathhouses built using locally sourced wood, is a hallmark of its charm. The facilities include three indoor baths, two outdoor baths, and two waterfalls for therapeutic bathing.

Retaining its traditional role as a {{ill|Toji|ja|湯治|1=thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toji|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}} (healing retreat), Kuroyu offers both a self-catering section and a standard inn, catering to long-term visitors and climbers. Neighboring {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Onsen|qq=|1=Magoroku Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448925}} is a short walk away, connected by a footbridge over the river.

Kuroyu is the only establishment in Nyuto Onsenkyō that closes during the winter, as the other six inns operate year-round.

The onsen inspired [[:en:Yoshiharu Tsuge|Yoshiharu Tsuge]], a famous manga artist, who visited in May 1969 and September 1976. He depicted its atmosphere in four illustrations titled "Scenery of Travels" and "Tōgenkō." <ref>"[http://www.tsugeyoshiharu.com/makita2.html Traveling with Yoshiharu Tsuge]"</ref>

== Access ==
* Railway
**** Take the JR [[:en:Akita Shinkansen|Akita Shinkansen]] to [[:en:Tazawako Station|Tazawako Station]], then take the Ugokotsu Bus "Nyuto Line" for approximately 55 minutes to the final stop, followed by a 20-minute walk.
* By car
**** From [[:en:Japan National Route 341|Route 341 (Japan)]], take {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 127|ja|秋田県道127号駒ケ岳線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 127|qq=|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 127|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595557}} and {{ill|Akita Prefectural Route 194|ja|秋田県道194号西山生保内線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Prefectural Route 194|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岩手県道・秋田県道194号西山生保内線|1=Iwate Prefectural Road and Akita Prefectural Road Route 194|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475027}}. At the Nyuto Onsenkyō Holiday Village, turn right and continue 1.3 km to the parking lot near the inn.

== Nearby Attractions ==
* [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsen]]
**** {{ill|Magoroku Onsen|ja|孫六温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Onsen|qq=|1=Magoroku Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448925}}
**** {{ill|Ipponmatsu Onsen|ja|一本松温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipponmatsu Onsen|qq=|1=Ipponmatsu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352778}}
**** {{ill|Kaniba Onsen|ja|蟹場温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaniba Onsen|qq=|1=Ganiba Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625863}}
**** {{ill|Ōgama Onsen|ja|大釜温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgama Onsen|qq=|1=Ōbuka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439784}}
**** {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen|qq=}}
